docs.title: Api
Architecture
docs.text:
Architecture

Deterministic system architecture for decision execution.

System Layers 
contracts/
packages/core/
registry/
api/
enforcement/

Each layer has a fixed responsibility. No overlap. No mutation.

Layer Responsibilities

Contracts Defines rules, inputs, outputs, and constraints. Core Engine Executes deterministic decision pipeline. Registry Loads and validates contracts. API Receives events and builds input payloads. Enforcement Applies decisions to GitHub (block / approve).

Execution Flow 
Event → Registry → Engine → Trace → Enforcement

Execution is linear, deterministic, and fail-fast.

Key Principles No layer overrides another Contract is the single source of truth No runtime mutation No external influence on decisions

Determinism Guarantee

The system ensures:

  • Same input → same output
  • No randomness
  • No hidden state
  • No external dependencies in decision logic

Why This Architecture Matters

Traditional systems mix logic, data, and enforcement. Manthan separates them cleanly, making decisions: traceable, auditable, and enforceable.

Summary

Manthan is built as a deterministic system where each layer has a single responsibility, ensuring consistent and reliable decision execution.

docs.title: Contracts
docs.text:
Contracts

Source of truth for all decisions in Manthan.

Purpose

Contracts define how decisions are made. They specify inputs, outputs, rules, and constraints. No decision exists outside a contract.

Structure 
intent
inputs
outputs
behavior
errorModel
determinism
boundaries
metadata

Each field has a defined role. No implicit behavior.

What Contracts Define What inputs are required What outputs are valid What rules must be enforced What behavior is allowed or forbidden

Validation

Every contract is validated before execution. Invalid contracts are rejected immediately.

Rules No runtime mutation No partial loading No implicit defaults Full contract always flows into trace

Determinism

Contracts explicitly define determinism constraints:

  • Allowed behaviors
  • Forbidden operations

This ensures decisions remain predictable and stable.

Boundaries

Contracts define system boundaries:

  • What data can be accessed
  • What operations are restricted

This prevents uncontrolled behavior.

Why Contracts Matter

Without contracts, decisions become:

  • Implicit
  • Inconsistent
  • Unverifiable

With contracts, decisions become:

  • Explicit
  • Deterministic
  • Auditable

Summary

Contracts define reality for decision-making in Manthan. They are the single source of truth.

docs.title: Decision Engine
Example Walkthrough
docs.text: This section demonstrates how Manthan produces deterministic decisions using real scenarios.

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 15000,
  "country": "high_risk",
  "user_verified": false
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
IF country == high_risk → reject

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → TRUE
  • First match → reject

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "amount threshold exceeded"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "amount": 2000,
  "country": "low_risk",
  "user_verified": true
}

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
IF amount > 10000 → reject
ELSE → approve

docs.title: Decision Flow
docs.text:
• amount > 10000 → FALSE
  • No reject rule matched → approve

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "reason": "within safe limits"
}

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "user_age": 17,
  "document_verified": false
}

docs.title: Graph
docs.text:
flowchart LR

A[Age Check] --> B[Eligibility]
A --> C[Document Verification]

B --> D[Decision]
C --> D

classDef node fill:#020617,stroke:#a78bfa,stroke-width:3px,color:#ffffff;
class A,B,C,D node;

docs.title: Logic
docs.text:
• user_age < 18 → not eligible
  • document_verified = false → fail

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "reason": "age and verification failure"
}

docs.title: Contract (v1.0)
docs.text:
rules:
  - condition: "score < 50"
    action: "reject"
  - condition: "score >= 50"
    action: "approve"

docs.title: Input
docs.text:
{
  "score": 72
}

docs.title: Evaluation
docs.text:
• score < 50 → FALSE
  • score >= 50 → TRUE

docs.title: Output
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "approve",
  "contract_version": "v1.0"
}

docs.title: Determinism Proof
docs.text:
For ALL examples:

Same Input → Same Rules → Same Output

No randomness No ambiguity No hidden state

docs.title: Key Takeaway
docs.text:
Manthan converts:

Inputs → Rules → Decisions

Into a fully deterministic system.

docs.title: Intelligence Layer
docs.text: Adds metadata without affecting decisions.

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
flowchart LR
    D[Decision] --> I[Intelligence] --> O[Output]

    classDef main fill:#020617,stroke:#60a5fa,stroke-width:2px,color:#ffffff;
    class D,I,O main;

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
{
  "decision": "reject",
  "score": 0.92,
  "confidence": "high"
}

docs.title: Behavior
docs.text:
• Enriches decision output with metadata
  • Does not modify or override the decision
  • Operates deterministically

docs.title: Rules
docs.text:
• MUST NOT change decision
  • Stateless
  • Deterministic

docs.title: Boundaries
docs.text:
• No decision authority
  • No side effects
  • No persistence

docs.title: Core Statement
docs.text: Explains decisions, never makes them.

docs.title: Papers
docs.text:
📖 Research

Foundations of deterministic decision systems.

Overview

Manthan is grounded in the idea that systems must not only compute, but make correct and enforceable decisions. This requires a shift from probabilistic generation to deterministic evaluation.

Papers

Explore the core research behind Manthan:

Manthan OS Foundational system design for decision infrastructure. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

Decision Auditability (Formal) Formal model for traceable and auditable decisions. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

Decision Auditability (Compliance) Applying auditability to real-world compliance systems. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

Decision Consistency & Enforcement Ensuring decisions remain consistent and enforceable. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

Distributed Decision Systems Extending decision infrastructure across distributed environments. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

AI Alignment via Decision Infrastructure Using deterministic decisions to improve AI system alignment. Read online   |   ⬇ Download

Philosophy

Modern systems rely on computation. Future systems must rely on correct decisions. Decisions must be deterministic, explainable, and enforceable.

Why This Matters

As systems become more autonomous, the cost of incorrect decisions increases. Without a deterministic decision layer:

  • Systems become unpredictable
  • Compliance breaks
  • Trust erodes

Manthan introduces decision infrastructure as a first-class system layer.

Summary

Manthan is not just a product. It is a shift in how systems are designed. From computation → to decision infrastructure.

docs.title: Philosophy
docs.text:
Manthan — Investor Memo

A first-principles view on why decision infrastructure is necessary.

1. Thesis

I believe modern software systems are missing a critical layer. We have built strong infrastructure for:

  • Computation (cloud systems)
  • Data integrity (databases)
  • Scalability (distributed systems)

But we have not built infrastructure for decision integrity. Manthan is built to solve that. It is decision infrastructure — a deterministic layer where decisions are traceable, auditable, and enforceable by design.

2. The Problem

Most modern systems — especially AI systems — operate in probabilistic generation. The same input can produce different outputs. There is no fixed reasoning path. There is no enforceable structure. This leads to fundamental issues:

  • Decisions are not predictable
  • Decisions are not traceable
  • Decisions are not enforceable

As systems become more autonomous, this becomes a structural risk.

3. Insight

The core issue is not intelligence. It is decision integrity. Systems today are designed to compute and generate. They are not designed to make correct, verifiable decisions. This is the gap Manthan addresses.

4. Solution: Manthan

Manthan is a deterministic decision operating system. Every decision flows through a fixed pipeline:

Contract → Engine → Trace → Enforcement
  • Contracts define decision logic explicitly
  • Engine evaluates deterministically (no randomness)
  • Trace explains how the decision was made
  • Enforcement applies decisions in real systems

Every decision is explicit. Nothing is hidden. Nothing changes at runtime.

5. Product (Current State)

The current system is deployed as a deterministic PR Gate. It evaluates pull requests against contracts and enforces outcomes. It can:

  • Block changes that violate defined rules
  • Provide full trace of evaluation
  • Explain decisions deterministically

The same input always produces the same result.

6. Why Now

Three shifts make this necessary:

  • AI systems are increasing decision volume
  • Regulation requires auditability and explainability
  • Autonomous systems require reliable decisions

Without a deterministic decision layer, these systems cannot be trusted.

7. Positioning

I do not see Manthan as an AI product or a developer tool. It is a foundational layer. We have:

  • Databases for data integrity
  • Cloud for compute
  • Manthan for decision integrity

8. Use Cases
  • Engineering enforcement systems (PR Gate)
  • Compliance and governance systems
  • Risk and policy evaluation
  • Autonomous system control layers

9. Differentiation Traditional Systems Manthan Probabilistic outputs Deterministic decisions Opaque reasoning Full traceability No enforcement Enforced outcomes Runtime variability Fixed evaluation pipeline

10. Vision

I see Manthan becoming the default decision layer in software systems. Where:

  • Every decision is contract-defined
  • Every evaluation is deterministic
  • Every outcome is auditable
  • Every action is enforceable

11. Closing

Manthan is not an incremental improvement. It is a shift. From systems that generate outputs to systems that make correct decisions.

docs.title: PR Gate
docs.text:
PR Gate

Enforced decision layer for GitHub pull requests.

What It Does Validates PRs against contracts Blocks unsafe or incomplete changes Explains decisions clearly Enforces outcomes automatically

How It Works 
Pull Request → Contract → Decision Engine → Enforcement

Every PR is evaluated deterministically before merge.

Example

A pull request without description: 

Decision: FAIL
Step: base_rules
Reason: missing PR description

Enforcement GitHub Check Runs Commit Status Pull Request Comments

Result No silent failures No ambiguity in reviews No bypassing rules Consistent decision enforcement

Why It Matters

Code review today is subjective and inconsistent. Manthan converts it into:

  • Deterministic validation
  • Traceable reasoning
  • Enforced outcomes

Install

Install Manthan PR Gate →

Summary

Manthan PR Gate ensures every pull request decision is: deterministic, explainable, and enforced.

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
Support — Manthan Support Deterministic Systems Require Deterministic Reports

Manthan enforces decisions as system truth. If something behaves unexpectedly, it must be reproducible.

🐞 Report Bug ⚙️ Contract Issue 💡 Feature Request Include: • Pull request link • Contract reference • Decision output • Trace Same input should produce the same result. If it does not, this is a system fault. Manthan — Decision Infrastructure

docs.title: System
docs.text:
System

Decision Infrastructure for deterministic systems.

Core Idea

Decisions are not suggestions. They are outcomes that must be enforced.

What Manthan Does Evaluates inputs against contracts Produces deterministic outcomes Generates full execution trace Enforces results automatically

System Flow 
Input → Contract → Engine → Trace → Enforcement

Each step is deterministic and fully auditable.

Core Properties Deterministic Traceable Auditable Enforceable

Why This Matters

Modern systems — especially AI-driven systems — lack deterministic decision layers. This leads to:

  • Inconsistent outcomes
  • Unverifiable reasoning
  • Broken compliance

Manthan introduces a new primitive: Decision Infrastructure

What Makes It Different No randomness No hidden logic No silent failures No optional enforcement

Summary

Manthan ensures that every decision in a system is: deterministic, traceable, and enforceable by design.
